─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Pytest itself is gone. What’s hanging is the wrapped command session, not the test runner, so I’m polling once more for the final buffered output before I decide whether to rerun in a
  simpler form.

• Waited for background terminal · python -m pytest tests -q

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• The wrapped session never returned its final status, so I’m rerunning the suite in fail-fast mode to get the concrete failure output directly. After that I’ll fix the break and rerun the
  full pass again.

• Ran python -m pytest tests -q -x

    =================================== FAILURES ===================================
    … +670 lines (ctrl + t to view transcript)
    !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! stopping after 1 failures !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• The direct pytest wrapper is unreliable here after the runner exits, so I’m switching to a simple subprocess capture to get a deterministic return code and failure trace.

• Working (8m 21s • esc to interrupt) · 1 background terminal running · /ps to view · /stop to close
